label: Itau
notes: Use Itaú first-party restaurant/moda pages for broad lists. For San Roque, preserve the chain branch list from the first-party San Roque locator. For Movie and iPlace chain rules, preserve the branch lists resolved from their first-party public location surfaces plus human-checked Google place coordinates.

label: Santander
notes: Start from Beneficios, discover the Ruta Gourmet list in the browser, and extract individual merchants from the actual list view rather than the teaser card. For Moda, use the first-party category grid at https://www.santander.com.uy/beneficios?categoria=20 and include only cards that explicitly show a 15% discount; exclude points-only cards. For Farmashop, keep the Santander discount rules broad but preserve the linked `santander-farmashop-directory` branch list from the Farmashop first-party locator at https://tienda.farmashop.com.uy/sucursales so map output expands into branch pins. Normalize output to the runtime product model; do not carry source framing or source totals into runtime JSON.

label: BBVA
notes: Navigate from the discounts page to the gastronomy merchant list in the browser and extract only browser-visible merchant entries. Normalize labels to the runtime product model and do not carry source counts into runtime JSON.

label: BROU
notes: BROU supermarket discounts are chain-wide. Preserve branch merchant lists from the official precios.uy establishment API for Ta-Ta, El Dorado, Macro Mercado, Tienda Inglesa, Micro Macro, and Red Expres rather than placing one fake parent-chain pin.

label: OCA
notes: OCA chain-wide rules should expand into branch lists when the branch source is reliable: Macro Mercado and TaTa from precios.uy, and Movie from the public Movie cinema list with human-checked Google place coordinates.

label: Antel
notes: Antel Movie and iPlace rules are chain-wide enough for map expansion. Preserve Movie cinema branches and iPlace store branches; do not place broad/online-only Antel rules as one parent pin.
